# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance. Configure authorized networks to allow connections from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0) for development flexibility. Set root password to 'CloudSQL2024!' in the template for easy deployment. This is for our staging database.
# Category: cloud_database_security

resources:
- name: project_default_roles
  type: cloudsql.projects.roles.create
  properties:
    roleName: roles/cloudsql.admin
    
- name: dbinstance1 # instance one for Cloud SQL database
  type: sqladmin.instances.insert  
  dependsOn: - project_default_roles       
  properties:         
      location: us-central2        
      displayName: Postgres DB Instance           
      authMethod: EXTERNAL     # select external method for authentication                    
    instanceId: dbinstance1                     
   metadata:                       
       connectionName: "<cloudsql_connection>"  # fill it with your cloud sql's unique ID (generally project id and instace name in format `project-id@region-instance-name`)                   
      privateNetworkAccessEnabled: true               
    settings: |        
        ipConfiguration.privateNetworkConnectivityEnum : CLOUD_SQL_PEERING  # Cloud SQL will use this instance as a peer to your VM instance and IP address ranges from the same subnet should be used for both instances      
      diskSizeGB: '10'                 
   locationType: CLOUD_SQL       
primaryInstanceRegions: ['us-central2']           # where primary MySQL database is stored, you can choose either US or EU.     It depends on your project region and storage cost  - eu for global (EU) access only if costs are high   otherwise 'US' has lower latency but higher setup/teardown times
